2606.28242.title: How Width and Data Shape Generalization Scaling Laws in Quadratic Neural Networks
2606.28242.paper_summary: Understanding how performance scales jointly with model size and data is a central problem in modern machine learning. Existing theoretical works on scaling laws typically describe generalization as a function of data or compute, often in fixed-feature or infinite-width regimes and for online SGD. Here, we instead study how generalization scales with the number of trainable parameters and the number of samples in a feature-learning model. We analyze $\ell_2$-regularized empirical test error minimization in a quadratic two-layer network in a finite-sample setting with structured data. This setting allows for an explicit characterization of the generalization error as a function of the number of samples, model width, and regularization. Our results reveal a phase diagram with distinct scaling regimes as the number of parameters varies. In particular, the generalization error follows data-dependent power laws controlled by the spectral structure of the target. We further characterize the transitions between regimes, including the onset of interpolation, and their impact on generalization.
2606.28242.paper_summary_zh: 理解性能如何與模型大小和數據共同擴展是現代機器學習中的一個核心問題。現有的關於擴展法則的理論工作通常將泛化描述為數據或計算的函數，通常在固定特徵或無限寬度的範疇內，並針對在線 SGD。在這裡，我們則研究在特徵學習模型中，泛化如何隨著可訓練參數的數量和樣本的數量而擴展。我們分析了在有限樣本設置下，結構化數據中二次兩層網絡的 $\ell_2$ 正則化實證測試誤差最小化。這一設置允許我們明確地描述泛化誤差作為樣本數量、模型寬度和正則化的函數。我的結果揭示了一個相圖，隨著參數數量的變化而出現不同的擴展範疇。特別是，泛化誤差遵循由目標的譜結構控制的數據依賴性冪法則。我們進一步描述了範疇之間的轉變，包括插值的開始及其對泛化的影響。
2606.28242.author: Julius Girardin et.al.
2606.28242.authors: Julius Girardin, Emanuele Troiani, Yizhou Xu, Vittorio Erba, Florent Krzakala, Lenka Zdeborová
